commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Then spoke Haggai the LORD'S messenger in the LORD'S message unto the people, saying, says the LORD. To excite the people's attention, Aggeus declares that he is sent by God, (Worthington) like the prophets of old. Some Jews have asserted (Calmet) that he, Malachi as, the Baptist, and Jacob, were angels incarnate. But let us leave (St. Jerome) these learned dreams. (Calmet) They have no better foundation than the ambiguity of malac, which signifies "an angel, or a messenger. "(Haydock) With you. This is often repeated, to encourage the dejected people. (Menochius).
